topic: The 2026 World Cup opener (Mexico vs South Africa) mirrors the 2010 opener — same teams, same date, 16 years apart — at the record-setting Estadio Azteca
subject: fifa-world-cup-trophy

scenes.voiceover: June eleventh, 2010 — Mexico and South Africa opened the World Cup. And they open it all over again today.

thumbnail.concept: Split frame: '2010' faded over a packed African stadium on the left, '2026' over the gold-lit Azteca on the right, Mexico and South Africa flags clashing in the center, huge gold 'JUNE 11' stamp.

youtube_title: Mexico vs South Africa Has Opened a World Cup Before | 2026 Opener
youtube_description: The 2026 World Cup kicks off June 11 with Mexico vs South Africa at the Estadio Azteca — and it's happened before. The exact same two teams opened the 2010 World Cup on the exact same date, June 11, ending 1-1 thanks to Siphiwe Tshabalala's iconic first goal of the tournament and Rafael Marquez's equalizer. Now the fixture moves to the Azteca, the only stadium ever to host three World Cups (1970, 1986, 2026). Same game, same date, 16 years apart. Will history repeat? Subscribe for a video every matchday. #WorldCup2026 #Mexico #SouthAfrica
instagram_caption: Same two teams. Same date. 16 years apart. Mexico vs South Africa opened the 2010 World Cup — and they open 2026 too, at the only stadium to host three World Cups. 2010 ended 1-1 — same scoreline today, or does El Tri finally win the opener? Drop your prediction 👇 Subscribe for a video every matchday. #tikitakafootytv
